basis: Token present in the node's own pre-existing query_axis.tsv query_terms / query_expansion_map.tsv expansions+synonyms -- i.e. the node's authoritative vocabulary that predates any Phase 3.9/3.11 patch.
rationale: Full weight: this is the node's own definition, not an after-the-fact addition.

basis: Hand-curated patch-added synonym that is reasonably construction-specific (low cross-node collision risk). Current members: cs255{complement,content,object}, cs262{complement,content}, cs387{perception,supplementary,accusative,verb,participle}, cs260{object,reported,speech}, cs378{duration,chronology}.
rationale: Patch vocabulary keeps strong weight but not full weight, since it was added after the node's original definition rather than being part of it.

basis: Broad patch-added synonym shared across many otherwise-unrelated nodes. Current sole member: cs378{time} (df=23/1032=2.2% of the ontology).
rationale: Keeps the node reachable for recall purposes without letting one generic, widely-shared word win the ranking on its own -- this is the direct mechanism fixing the reported "cs378: time系ノードの過剰ヒット(45件)" complaint.

tier_or_scope: secondary & contextual tiers only (never applied to primary)
weight: multiplier by document-frequency band, measured against the live 1032-node ontology: df<=5 nodes -> x1.00 (rare); df 6-15 -> x0.85 (common); df>=16 -> x0.70 (very common)
basis: IDF-style discount so a shared patch term contributes less than a rare, discriminating one. Measured: "time" df=23 -> x0.70; "object" df=27, "accusative" df=24 -> x0.70; "complement" df=7 -> x0.85; "verb" df=16 -> x0.70; "participle" df=11 -> x0.85; "content","perception","reported","speech","supplementary","duration","chronology" df<=5 -> x1.00. Primary-tier tokens are exempt so a node's own canonical label is never penalized for containing a common word (e.g. cs42 "Genitive of Time" is not punished for "time" being common).

tier_or_scope: tie-break only, applied after the weighted score comparison
weight: +(candidate's own, unchanged query_axis.tsv "clause" axis weight) x 0.25
basis: Rewards clause-level/constructional categories over bare nominal/case categories when weighted scores already tie. Grounded check: cs378 carries clause=0.70 (bonus +0.175); cs397/cs403/cs415 carry clause=1.00/1.00/1.00 (bonus +0.25); cs13/cs42/cs67 (Nominative/Genitive/Dative for Time) carry no clause axis at all (bonus +0.00). Implements the user's "axis一致度優先" tie-break request using axis weights that already exist in query_axis.tsv -- no axis value is changed.
Implements "canonical label優先": prefers the canonical entry over an alias when two candidates are still tied after scoring and axis alignment.

weight: max 5 secondary-tier terms AND max 3 contextual-tier terms per node
basis: Implements "expansion limit per axis": caps how many broad/generic synonyms any single node may accumulate, preventing recurrence of the over-broad-expansion problem that produced the cs378 flooding case. Current usage against the cap: cs255 3/5 secondary; cs262 2/5; cs387 5/5 secondary (AT CAP -- see synonym_priority_map.tsv); cs260 3/5; cs378 2/5 secondary + 1/3 contextual. No node currently exceeds its cap.

weight: every matched token keeps a strictly positive contribution (minimum value after the heaviest correction band: 0.30 x 0.70 = 0.21)
basis: No tier or correction may zero out a token's contribution. Verified by simulation: re-running the full 22-query battery from rerun_test_report.tsv against this weighting scheme produced target_absent=0 and zero_candidates=0 across all 22 (see disambiguation_rules.tsv rows 7-8 for the one regression this check caught and the fix applied).
